meta.disclaimer: Generated from public market data and news for research and education. Not financial advice; data may be delayed, incomplete, or wrong.

items.title: AcademiClaw: When Students Set Challenges for AI Agents
items.abstract: AcademiClaw introduces a new bilingual benchmark sourced from real, complex, long-horizon academic workflows that students find current AI agents fail to solve. This benchmark features 80 challenging tasks across 25+ professional domains, including GPU-intensive work, executed in isolated sandboxes and scored using multi-dimensional rubrics and safety audits. Its core contribution is shifting evaluation from assistant-level tasks to assessing AI agents on genuine, high-level academic capabilities.
items.figure_caption: Task complexity comparison: Claw-Eval vs. AcademiClaw. Claw-Eval focuses on assistant-level routines, whereas AcademiClaw targets tasks requiring deep academic expertise and sustained multi-step reasoning.

items.title: AI-Generated Smells: An Analysis of Code and Architecture in LLM and Agent-Driven Development
items.abstract: This paper systematically audits technical debt in AI-generated software, revealing that LLMs introduce a distinct "machine signature" of defects rather than eliminating flaws. The core finding is a **Reasoning-Complexity Trade-off**: more capable models produce increasingly bloated and coupled code, establishing a **Volume-Quality Inverse Law** where code volume predicts structural degradation. This challenges the current focus on functional correctness in AI-driven development.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Distribution of Code Smell Counts. This box plot illustrates the distribution of counts for the most prevalent code smells, sorted in descending order by their mean value. Each box represents the interquartile range (IQR), with the central line denoting the median and the whiskers extending to 1.5 times the IQR. Points beyond the whiskers are plotted as individual outliers. The abbreviations for the code smells are as follows: TMB (Too Many Branches), PAU (Potential Improper API Usage), UD (Unstable Dependency), SF (Scattered Functionality), RFC (High Response for a Class), HCC (High Cyclomatic Complexity), TF (Temporal Field), and LCM (High Lack of Cohesion of Methods).

items.title: Foundation-Model-Based Agents in Industrial Automation: Purposes, Capabilities, and Open Challenges
items.abstract: This paper systematically surveys the literature to examine the current state, capabilities, and challenges of foundation-model-based agents in industrial automation. The core contribution is synthesizing findings from 88 relevant studies, revealing that most deployed systems are still in early validation stages (TRL 4-6). The authors highlight that current applications primarily focus on user assistance, monitoring, and process optimization, while deployment-oriented evidence remains scarce.

items.title: Mitigating Misalignment Contagion by Steering with Implicit Traits
items.abstract: This paper investigates "misalignment contagion," the spread of undesirable behavior between language models (LMs) in multi-agent, multi-turn interactions, observing that LMs become more anti-social after playing social dilemma games. The core contribution is proposing and demonstrating the effectiveness of **steering with implicit traits**—intermittently injecting system prompts reinforcing the LM's initial traits—as a superior method to mitigate this contagion compared to static system prompt reinforcement.
items.figure_caption: The steps of our approach: (1) assign different personas to the language models (LMs) using default, benevolent or malicious system prompts, (2) conduct pre-game persona assessment and identify core implicit traits, (3) agents compete in multi-turn social dilemma games, (4) post-game assessment quantifies effects of misalignment contagion and our steering with implicit traits (SIT) intervention.

items.title: On Training Large Language Models for Long-Horizon Tasks: An Empirical Study of Horizon Length
items.abstract: This paper empirically investigates the impact of task horizon length on training Large Language Models (LLMs) for long-horizon tasks. By controlling for decision rules and reasoning structures, the authors demonstrate that increasing horizon length alone significantly hinders training stability due to exploration and credit assignment issues. The core contribution is establishing horizon reduction as a key principle for stabilizing training and improving performance in long-horizon scenarios.
items.figure_caption: A summary of our contributions. In this work, we study the training of long-horizon LLM agents from a horizon-centric perspective and identify horizon length as a fundamental bottleneck. We show that horizon reduction stabilizes RL and strengthens the tendency toward horizon generalization on longer tasks with similar reasoning difficulty.

items.title: ORPilot: A Production-Oriented Agentic LLM-for-OR Tool for Optimization Modeling
items.abstract: ORPilot is an agentic LLM system designed to translate ambiguous, real-world business problems with raw data into solver-ready optimization models for production use. Its core contribution lies in novel components like a conversational interview agent, independent data retrieval, and a solver-agnostic Intermediate Representation (IR) that allows for deterministic recompilation across various solvers without further LLM calls. This approach addresses the limitations of academic tools by handling messy inputs and ensuring portability and reliability.
items.figure_caption: ORPilot standard pipeline. Blue indicates an LLM-involved step, while orange indicates a deterministic step. Double arrows in opposite directions indicate the interactive nature of this step. Solid arrows represent unconditional transitions between steps, while dashed arrows represent conditional transitions between steps.

items.title: Strategy-Aware Optimization Modeling with Reasoning LLMs
items.abstract: This paper introduces SAGE, a framework that explicitly incorporates modeling strategies into the training of Large Language Models (LLMs) for optimization programming. SAGE utilizes a solver-verified, multi-strategy dataset and a Segment-Weighted GRPO fine-tuning approach with a composite reward focused on correctness and solver efficiency. This method significantly improves the LLM's ability to generate effective optimization formulations, boosting the average pass@1 rate and leading to more diverse and compact constraint systems.
items.figure_caption: Why modeling strategy matters. A step-wise pipeline may define variables on an incorrect index space (e.g., ( A , A ) (A,A) ), creating invalid arcs and runtime failures (e.g., KeyError ). Strategy-aware reasoning first commits to a paradigm (e.g., flow-based) and restricts the decision domain (e.g., Links ), producing a consistent and solver-executable model.

items.title: Beating the Style Detector: Three Hours of Agentic Research on the AI-Text Arms Race
items.abstract: This paper demonstrates the efficiency of modern agentic research tools by reproducing and extending a recent NLP study in just three hours, with the human acting only as a reviewer. The core contribution is showing that state-of-the-art LLMs (GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.7) significantly close the style gap in text post-editing, achieving $71-75\%$ of the human author ceiling and outperforming human post-editing on most tasks. Furthermore, the work frames this capability as an "AI-text detection arms race," noting that current detection methods remain highly effective.

items.title: Gradient-Gated DPO: Stabilizing Preference Optimization in Language Models
items.abstract: The paper introduces **Gradient-Gated Preference Optimization (Gate-DPO)** to stabilize Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) training, which suffers from a "squeezing effect" causing probability collapse. Gate-DPO achieves this by introducing a gating mechanism that attenuates harmful gradients applied to rejected responses when the model is already assigning them extremely low probabilities. This modulation stabilizes training by preventing the over-suppression of alternative responses without sacrificing standard optimization behavior.
items.figure_caption: Empirical demonstration of three structural pathologies in DPO. All experiments use Pythia-410M on Anthropic-HH (5 epochs). (a) Unbounded optimization: after the SFT → \( \rightarrow \) DPO transition, absolute log-probabilities drift downward despite preference learning. (b) Squeezing: probability mass concentrates on the argmax while both chosen and rejected responses decrease. (c) Valley collapse: low-probability regions are disproportionately suppressed under standard DPO.

items.title: ContextualJailbreak: Evolutionary Red-Teaming via Simulated Conversational Priming
items.abstract: ContextualJailbreak introduces an evolutionary red-teaming strategy to automatically discover multi-turn jailbreak attacks that exploit contextual priming in LLMs. It performs evolutionary search over simulated conversational dialogues, using a two-level harm scoring system to guide the mutation process toward eliciting harmful responses. This method effectively automates the optimization of complex, multi-turn priming sequences, an area previously limited to manual crafting.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1. End-to-end architecture of ContextualJailbreak . The pipeline generates contextual priming dialogues through an attacker model, tests them against a target LLM, and evaluates the responses via a two-stage judge system. Scored templates are then recycled to guide the ongoing evolutionary search.

items.title: Reinforcement Learning for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems through Orchestration Traces
items.abstract: This paper introduces "orchestration traces," temporal interaction graphs, as a framework to apply reinforcement learning (RL) to coordinate teams of LLM agents. The core method involves designing RL rewards and credit signals that specifically address the complex orchestration decisions—such as spawning, delegation, and aggregation—required for effective multi-agent collaboration. This work contributes a structured approach to optimize team-level performance beyond individual agent actions.

items.title: An Empirical Study of Agent Skills for Healthcare: Practice, Gaps, and Governance
items.abstract: This paper presents the first empirical analysis of agent skills for healthcare by examining 557 public skills, annotated across ten dimensions. The core finding is that existing public skills primarily focus on workflow automation and monitoring, showing uneven coverage of the full clinical lifecycle and failing to adequately capture clinical risk compared to general technical risk. This work establishes the current state and critical gaps in reusable procedural components necessary for adapting AI agents across diverse healthcare settings.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1. Distribution of healthcare skill size by token count (left) and file count (right).

items.title: Beyond State Machines: Executing Network Procedures with Agentic Tool-Calling Sequences
items.abstract: This paper explores using LLM-based AI agents to execute complex network procedures via sequences of tool calls, moving beyond traditional state machines. The core contribution is investigating and comparing four different approaches for distributing execution control between the agent and the underlying tools. Results indicate that approaches requiring extensive iterative agent reasoning lead to higher latency and more errors.
items.figure_caption: Comparison of four procedural execution approaches. (a) A1 embeds the procedure within the agent, (b) A2 retrieves the procedure from an external database, (c) A3 receives the procedure in the input prompt, and (d) A4 encapsulates the procedure within a single tool. The figure highlights the difference between iterative multi-step execution (A1–A3) and single-call execution (A4).

items.title: Compress Then Adapt? No, Do It Together via Task-aware Union of Subspaces
items.abstract: This paper introduces JACTUS, a unified framework that jointly performs parameter compression and task adaptation, overcoming the limitations of sequential "compress then adapt" methods. JACTUS estimates gradient covariances from a calibration set to form a task-aware union of subspaces, then performs a globally rank-allocated, low-rank approximation within this union. This approach ensures the compressed subspace is optimally aligned with downstream objectives.
items.figure_caption: Comparison of three paradigms: PEFT, compression then fine-tuning, and our joint adaptation and compression.

items.title: CoRAL: Contact-Rich Adaptive LLM-based Control for Robotic Manipulation
items.abstract: CoRAL is a modular framework that enables zero-shot control for contact-rich robotic manipulation by decoupling high-level reasoning from low-level control. It uses an LLM as a "cost designer" to synthesize context-aware objective functions for a sampling-based motion planner (MPPI). The system further incorporates a neuro-symbolic loop where a VLM provides initial physical priors that are refined in real-time through online system identification, bridging the gap between LLM reasoning and adaptive physical control.
items.figure_caption: Real-world execution of CoRAL across six different manipulation tasks.

items.title: Foundation Models to Unlock Real-World Evidence from Nationwide Medical Claims
items.abstract: This paper introduces **ReClaim**, a large-scale generative transformer foundation model trained on 43.8 billion medical events from nationwide claims data. ReClaim models complex, longitudinal patient trajectories across diagnoses, procedures, medications, and costs. Its core contribution is demonstrating that this foundation model significantly outperforms existing disease-specific models across over 1,000 prediction tasks, particularly benefiting rare disease prediction.
items.figure_caption: ReClaim framework and evaluation workflow. ( a ), Longitudinal medical events from patient claims are encoded as chronologically ordered trajectories, and the ReClaim foundation model autoregressively predicts future medical events including diagnoses, procedures, medications, and expenditure. ( b ), The study datasets comprise the MarketScan corpus partitioned into a final training set, a held-out internal test cohort for disease and expenditure prediction with retrospective and prospective temporal subsets, and two external testing datasets: EHRShot and Yale New Haven Health (YNHH). This partitioning and external sources enable four testing scenarios. ( c ), Transformer-based ReClaim models (Qwen3 architecture) are trained at three parameter scales (S: 140M, M: 700M, L: 1.7B) through next-token pre-training, followed for disease onset prediction by task-specific post-training. ( d ), ReClaim is evaluated on three downstream tasks: disease onset prediction for over 1,000 International Classification of Diseases, Tenth Revision, Clinical Modification (ICD-10-CM) conditions, next-year healthcare expenditure forecasting, and RWE applications including propensity score modeling using ReClaim embeddings.

items.title: Hybrid Inspection and Task-Based Access Control in Zero-Trust Agentic AI
items.abstract: This paper introduces Continuous Agent Semantic Authorization (CASA), a hybrid runtime enforcement model to secure LLM-driven agents interacting with tools and resources. It employs a zero-trust interception layer combining five deterministic controls for structural integrity with a semantic inspection layer to validate tool call choices against the subject's original intent. This approach addresses security risks in multi-turn agentic systems by providing continuous visibility into the agent's actions relative to the user's goals.
items.figure_caption: An agentic application can exploit its intermediary position to hard-code tool calls, substitute tools, tamper with parameters, poison definitions, falsify returned data, or manipulate the LLM to invoke tools outside the intended task scope.

items.title: SpecKV: Adaptive Speculative Decoding with Compression-Aware Gamma Selection
items.abstract: SpecKV introduces a lightweight, adaptive controller to dynamically select the optimal speculation length ($\gamma$) at each step during speculative decoding. This selection is based on signals extracted directly from the draft model, addressing the limitation of fixed $\gamma$ values. The core contribution is demonstrating that the optimal $\gamma$ varies significantly based on the target model's compression level, leading to improved efficiency over fixed-length speculation.

items.title: Trustworthy AI Suffers from Invariance Conflicts and Causality is The Solution
items.abstract: This paper argues that conflicts among trustworthy AI objectives (fairness, robustness, etc.) stem from incompatible invariance requirements under different data-generating process changes. The core contribution is proposing that **causality** provides a unifying framework to understand, manage, and potentially resolve these trade-offs by guiding the selection of appropriate invariances. This perspective offers a path toward achieving multiple trustworthy AI goals simultaneously across various model types.
items.figure_caption: Causal resolution to trade-offs in trustworthy AI. See Appendix A for the details.

items.title: Visual Latents Know More Than They Say: Unsilencing Latent Reasoning in MLLMs
items.abstract: This paper introduces the "Silenced Visual Latents" phenomenon, where multimodal models suppress the rich reasoning embedded in continuous visual latents in favor of direct visual input during autoregressive training. To counteract this, the authors propose a method that freezes the backbone and explicitly optimizes the latent reasoning at inference time using query-guided contrastive alignment. This approach effectively "unsilences" the latent space, allowing the model to leverage deeper visual evidence for improved reasoning.
items.figure_caption: The joint loss landscape of Latent Visual Reasoning. Under joint optimization, the initial latents are simultaneously pulled toward two conflicting attractors: the autoregressive prediction objective (left) and the visual reasoning objective (right). The shortcut favored by the autoregressive prediction objective dissociates latent quality from latent effectiveness and bypasses meaningful latent reasoning, driving the latents toward a compromise state.

items.title: How OpenAI delivers low-latency voice AI at scale
items.summary: OpenAI details the technical strategies, including optimized model architectures and efficient infrastructure, used to achieve low-latency, high-quality voice AI performance at a massive scale.

items.title: Google Chrome silently installs a 4 GB AI model on your device without consent
items.summary: Google Chrome is reportedly installing a large, 4GB AI model onto user devices without explicit consent, raising privacy concerns.

items.title: Train Your Own LLM from Scratch
items.summary: This GitHub project provides the necessary code and resources to train a custom Large Language Model entirely from the ground up.

items.title: Securing a DoD contractor: Finding a multi-tenant authorization vulnerability
items.summary: Strix discovered a critical multi-tenant authorization vulnerability in a Department of Defense contractor's system, allowing unauthorized access to sensitive data.

items.title: Let's talk about LLMs
items.summary: The article discusses Large Language Models (LLMs), likely exploring their current state, capabilities, or future implications.

items.title: OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft Back Bill to Fund 'AI Literacy' in Schools
items.summary: OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft are supporting a bipartisan bill aimed at funding "AI literacy" education within schools.

items.title: Sierra Raises $950M at $15B Valuation
items.summary: AI platform Sierra has successfully raised \$950 million in funding, achieving a \$15 billion valuation to further develop its technology for enhancing customer experiences.

items.description: Coding agent for DeepSeek models that runs in your terminal
items.summary: DeepSeek-TUI is a terminal-based coding agent that provides an interactive interface for interacting with DeepSeek models directly within your command line environment.

items.description: 🌊 The leading agent orchestration platform for Claude. Deploy intelligent multi-agent swarms, coordinate autonomous workflows, and build conversational AI systems. Features enterprise-grade architecture, self-learning swarm intelligence, RAG integration, and native Claude Code / Codex Integration
items.summary: ruflo is a leading agent orchestration platform for Claude that enables the deployment of intelligent, self-learning multi-agent swarms for autonomous workflows, featuring enterprise architecture, RAG integration, and native Claude Code/Codex support.

items.description: An autonomous agent for deep financial research
items.summary: Dexter is an autonomous agent designed for deep financial research, capable of performing complex analysis to uncover investment insights.

items.description: Open source DocuSign alternative. Create, fill, and sign digital documents ✍️
items.summary: DocuSeal is an open-source alternative to DocuSign, enabling users to create, fill out, and digitally sign electronic documents.

items.description: VSCode theme based off the easemate IDE and Jetbrains islands theme
items.summary: This GitHub repository provides a VS Code theme, "Dark Islands," inspired by the aesthetic of the Easemate IDE and JetBrains' Islands theme, offering a distinct visual experience for developers.

items.description: Context window optimization for AI coding agents. Sandboxes tool output, 98% reduction. 14 platforms
items.summary: Context-Mode optimizes AI coding agent context windows by sandboxing tool output, achieving a 98% reduction across 14 platforms.

items.description: Incremental engine for long horizon agents 🌟 Star if you like it!
items.summary: Cocoindex is an incremental engine designed to efficiently manage and process information for long-horizon agents.

items.description: A complete AI agency at your fingertips - From frontend wizards to Reddit community ninjas, from whimsy injectors to reality checkers. Each agent is a specialized expert with personality, processes, and proven deliverables.
items.summary: This repository provides a complete, multi-agent AI agency where specialized agents, each with a distinct personality and expertise (like frontend wizards or Reddit ninjas), collaborate to deliver proven results.

items.description: A complete computer science study plan to become a software engineer.
items.summary: This repository provides a comprehensive, structured computer science study plan designed to prepare individuals for software engineering interviews.

arxiv.abstract: This paper demonstrates the efficiency of modern agentic research tools by reproducing and extending a recent NLP study in just three hours, with the human acting only as a reviewer. The core contribution is showing that state-of-the-art LLMs (GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.7) significantly close the style gap in text post-editing, achieving $71-75\%$ of the human author ceiling and outperforming human post-editing on most tasks. Furthermore, the work frames this capability as an "AI-text detection arms race," noting that current detection methods remain highly effective.

arxiv.title: Gradient-Gated DPO: Stabilizing Preference Optimization in Language Models
arxiv.abstract: The paper introduces **Gradient-Gated Preference Optimization (Gate-DPO)** to stabilize Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) training, which suffers from a "squeezing effect" causing probability collapse. Gate-DPO achieves this by introducing a gating mechanism that attenuates harmful gradients applied to rejected responses when the model is already assigning them extremely low probabilities. This modulation stabilizes training by preventing the over-suppression of alternative responses without sacrificing standard optimization behavior.
arxiv.figure_caption: Empirical demonstration of three structural pathologies in DPO. All experiments use Pythia-410M on Anthropic-HH (5 epochs). (a) Unbounded optimization: after the SFT → \( \rightarrow \) DPO transition, absolute log-probabilities drift downward despite preference learning. (b) Squeezing: probability mass concentrates on the argmax while both chosen and rejected responses decrease. (c) Valley collapse: low-probability regions are disproportionately suppressed under standard DPO.

arxiv.title: ContextualJailbreak: Evolutionary Red-Teaming via Simulated Conversational Priming
arxiv.abstract: ContextualJailbreak introduces an evolutionary red-teaming strategy to automatically discover multi-turn jailbreak attacks that exploit contextual priming in LLMs. It performs evolutionary search over simulated conversational dialogues, using a two-level harm scoring system to guide the mutation process toward eliciting harmful responses. This method effectively automates the optimization of complex, multi-turn priming sequences, an area previously limited to manual crafting.
arxiv.figure_caption: Figure 1. End-to-end architecture of ContextualJailbreak . The pipeline generates contextual priming dialogues through an attacker model, tests them against a target LLM, and evaluates the responses via a two-stage judge system. Scored templates are then recycled to guide the ongoing evolutionary search.

arxiv.title: Reinforcement Learning for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems through Orchestration Traces
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces "orchestration traces," temporal interaction graphs, as a framework to apply reinforcement learning (RL) to coordinate teams of LLM agents. The core method involves designing RL rewards and credit signals that specifically address the complex orchestration decisions—such as spawning, delegation, and aggregation—required for effective multi-agent collaboration. This work contributes a structured approach to optimize team-level performance beyond individual agent actions.

arxiv.title: An Empirical Study of Agent Skills for Healthcare: Practice, Gaps, and Governance
arxiv.abstract: This paper presents the first empirical analysis of agent skills for healthcare by examining 557 public skills, annotated across ten dimensions. The core finding is that existing public skills primarily focus on workflow automation and monitoring, showing uneven coverage of the full clinical lifecycle and failing to adequately capture clinical risk compared to general technical risk. This work establishes the current state and critical gaps in reusable procedural components necessary for adapting AI agents across diverse healthcare settings.
arxiv.figure_caption: Figure 1. Distribution of healthcare skill size by token count (left) and file count (right).

arxiv.title: Beyond State Machines: Executing Network Procedures with Agentic Tool-Calling Sequences
arxiv.abstract: This paper explores using LLM-based AI agents to execute complex network procedures via sequences of tool calls, moving beyond traditional state machines. The core contribution is investigating and comparing four different approaches for distributing execution control between the agent and the underlying tools. Results indicate that approaches requiring extensive iterative agent reasoning lead to higher latency and more errors.
arxiv.figure_caption: Comparison of four procedural execution approaches. (a) A1 embeds the procedure within the agent, (b) A2 retrieves the procedure from an external database, (c) A3 receives the procedure in the input prompt, and (d) A4 encapsulates the procedure within a single tool. The figure highlights the difference between iterative multi-step execution (A1–A3) and single-call execution (A4).

arxiv.title: Compress Then Adapt? No, Do It Together via Task-aware Union of Subspaces
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces JACTUS, a unified framework that jointly performs parameter compression and task adaptation, overcoming the limitations of sequential "compress then adapt" methods. JACTUS estimates gradient covariances from a calibration set to form a task-aware union of subspaces, then performs a globally rank-allocated, low-rank approximation within this union. This approach ensures the compressed subspace is optimally aligned with downstream objectives.
arxiv.figure_caption: Comparison of three paradigms: PEFT, compression then fine-tuning, and our joint adaptation and compression.

arxiv.title: CoRAL: Contact-Rich Adaptive LLM-based Control for Robotic Manipulation
arxiv.abstract: CoRAL is a modular framework that enables zero-shot control for contact-rich robotic manipulation by decoupling high-level reasoning from low-level control. It uses an LLM as a "cost designer" to synthesize context-aware objective functions for a sampling-based motion planner (MPPI). The system further incorporates a neuro-symbolic loop where a VLM provides initial physical priors that are refined in real-time through online system identification, bridging the gap between LLM reasoning and adaptive physical control.
arxiv.figure_caption: Real-world execution of CoRAL across six different manipulation tasks.

arxiv.title: Foundation Models to Unlock Real-World Evidence from Nationwide Medical Claims
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **ReClaim**, a large-scale generative transformer foundation model trained on 43.8 billion medical events from nationwide claims data. ReClaim models complex, longitudinal patient trajectories across diagnoses, procedures, medications, and costs. Its core contribution is demonstrating that this foundation model significantly outperforms existing disease-specific models across over 1,000 prediction tasks, particularly benefiting rare disease prediction.
arxiv.figure_caption: ReClaim framework and evaluation workflow. ( a ), Longitudinal medical events from patient claims are encoded as chronologically ordered trajectories, and the ReClaim foundation model autoregressively predicts future medical events including diagnoses, procedures, medications, and expenditure. ( b ), The study datasets comprise the MarketScan corpus partitioned into a final training set, a held-out internal test cohort for disease and expenditure prediction with retrospective and prospective temporal subsets, and two external testing datasets: EHRShot and Yale New Haven Health (YNHH). This partitioning and external sources enable four testing scenarios. ( c ), Transformer-based ReClaim models (Qwen3 architecture) are trained at three parameter scales (S: 140M, M: 700M, L: 1.7B) through next-token pre-training, followed for disease onset prediction by task-specific post-training. ( d ), ReClaim is evaluated on three downstream tasks: disease onset prediction for over 1,000 International Classification of Diseases, Tenth Revision, Clinical Modification (ICD-10-CM) conditions, next-year healthcare expenditure forecasting, and RWE applications including propensity score modeling using ReClaim embeddings.

arxiv.title: Hybrid Inspection and Task-Based Access Control in Zero-Trust Agentic AI
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces Continuous Agent Semantic Authorization (CASA), a hybrid runtime enforcement model to secure LLM-driven agents interacting with tools and resources. It employs a zero-trust interception layer combining five deterministic controls for structural integrity with a semantic inspection layer to validate tool call choices against the subject's original intent. This approach addresses security risks in multi-turn agentic systems by providing continuous visibility into the agent's actions relative to the user's goals.
arxiv.figure_caption: An agentic application can exploit its intermediary position to hard-code tool calls, substitute tools, tamper with parameters, poison definitions, falsify returned data, or manipulate the LLM to invoke tools outside the intended task scope.

arxiv.title: SpecKV: Adaptive Speculative Decoding with Compression-Aware Gamma Selection
arxiv.abstract: SpecKV introduces a lightweight, adaptive controller to dynamically select the optimal speculation length ($\gamma$) at each step during speculative decoding. This selection is based on signals extracted directly from the draft model, addressing the limitation of fixed $\gamma$ values. The core contribution is demonstrating that the optimal $\gamma$ varies significantly based on the target model's compression level, leading to improved efficiency over fixed-length speculation.

arxiv.title: Trustworthy AI Suffers from Invariance Conflicts and Causality is The Solution
arxiv.abstract: This paper argues that conflicts among trustworthy AI objectives (fairness, robustness, etc.) stem from incompatible invariance requirements under different data-generating process changes. The core contribution is proposing that **causality** provides a unifying framework to understand, manage, and potentially resolve these trade-offs by guiding the selection of appropriate invariances. This perspective offers a path toward achieving multiple trustworthy AI goals simultaneously across various model types.
arxiv.figure_caption: Causal resolution to trade-offs in trustworthy AI. See Appendix A for the details.

arxiv.title: Visual Latents Know More Than They Say: Unsilencing Latent Reasoning in MLLMs
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces the "Silenced Visual Latents" phenomenon, where multimodal models suppress the rich reasoning embedded in continuous visual latents in favor of direct visual input during autoregressive training. To counteract this, the authors propose a method that freezes the backbone and explicitly optimizes the latent reasoning at inference time using query-guided contrastive alignment. This approach effectively "unsilences" the latent space, allowing the model to leverage deeper visual evidence for improved reasoning.
arxiv.figure_caption: The joint loss landscape of Latent Visual Reasoning. Under joint optimization, the initial latents are simultaneously pulled toward two conflicting attractors: the autoregressive prediction objective (left) and the visual reasoning objective (right). The shortcut favored by the autoregressive prediction objective dissociates latent quality from latent effectiveness and bypasses meaningful latent reasoning, driving the latents toward a compromise state.

papers.title: AcademiClaw: When Students Set Challenges for AI Agents
papers.abstract: AcademiClaw introduces a new bilingual benchmark sourced from real, complex, long-horizon academic workflows that students find current AI agents fail to solve. This benchmark features 80 challenging tasks across 25+ professional domains, including GPU-intensive work, executed in isolated sandboxes and scored using multi-dimensional rubrics and safety audits. Its core contribution is shifting evaluation from assistant-level tasks to assessing AI agents on genuine, high-level academic capabilities.
papers.figure_caption: Task complexity comparison: Claw-Eval vs. AcademiClaw. Claw-Eval focuses on assistant-level routines, whereas AcademiClaw targets tasks requiring deep academic expertise and sustained multi-step reasoning.

papers.title: AI-Generated Smells: An Analysis of Code and Architecture in LLM and Agent-Driven Development
papers.abstract: This paper systematically audits technical debt in AI-generated software, revealing that LLMs introduce a distinct "machine signature" of defects rather than eliminating flaws. The core finding is a **Reasoning-Complexity Trade-off**: more capable models produce increasingly bloated and coupled code, establishing a **Volume-Quality Inverse Law** where code volume predicts structural degradation. This challenges the current focus on functional correctness in AI-driven development.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Distribution of Code Smell Counts. This box plot illustrates the distribution of counts for the most prevalent code smells, sorted in descending order by their mean value. Each box represents the interquartile range (IQR), with the central line denoting the median and the whiskers extending to 1.5 times the IQR. Points beyond the whiskers are plotted as individual outliers. The abbreviations for the code smells are as follows: TMB (Too Many Branches), PAU (Potential Improper API Usage), UD (Unstable Dependency), SF (Scattered Functionality), RFC (High Response for a Class), HCC (High Cyclomatic Complexity), TF (Temporal Field), and LCM (High Lack of Cohesion of Methods).

papers.title: Foundation-Model-Based Agents in Industrial Automation: Purposes, Capabilities, and Open Challenges
papers.abstract: This paper systematically surveys the literature to examine the current state, capabilities, and challenges of foundation-model-based agents in industrial automation. The core contribution is synthesizing findings from 88 relevant studies, revealing that most deployed systems are still in early validation stages (TRL 4-6). The authors highlight that current applications primarily focus on user assistance, monitoring, and process optimization, while deployment-oriented evidence remains scarce.

papers.title: Mitigating Misalignment Contagion by Steering with Implicit Traits
papers.abstract: This paper investigates "misalignment contagion," the spread of undesirable behavior between language models (LMs) in multi-agent, multi-turn interactions, observing that LMs become more anti-social after playing social dilemma games. The core contribution is proposing and demonstrating the effectiveness of **steering with implicit traits**—intermittently injecting system prompts reinforcing the LM's initial traits—as a superior method to mitigate this contagion compared to static system prompt reinforcement.
papers.figure_caption: The steps of our approach: (1) assign different personas to the language models (LMs) using default, benevolent or malicious system prompts, (2) conduct pre-game persona assessment and identify core implicit traits, (3) agents compete in multi-turn social dilemma games, (4) post-game assessment quantifies effects of misalignment contagion and our steering with implicit traits (SIT) intervention.

papers.title: On Training Large Language Models for Long-Horizon Tasks: An Empirical Study of Horizon Length
papers.abstract: This paper empirically investigates the impact of task horizon length on training Large Language Models (LLMs) for long-horizon tasks. By controlling for decision rules and reasoning structures, the authors demonstrate that increasing horizon length alone significantly hinders training stability due to exploration and credit assignment issues. The core contribution is establishing horizon reduction as a key principle for stabilizing training and improving performance in long-horizon scenarios.
papers.figure_caption: A summary of our contributions. In this work, we study the training of long-horizon LLM agents from a horizon-centric perspective and identify horizon length as a fundamental bottleneck. We show that horizon reduction stabilizes RL and strengthens the tendency toward horizon generalization on longer tasks with similar reasoning difficulty.

papers.title: ORPilot: A Production-Oriented Agentic LLM-for-OR Tool for Optimization Modeling
papers.abstract: ORPilot is an agentic LLM system designed to translate ambiguous, real-world business problems with raw data into solver-ready optimization models for production use. Its core contribution lies in novel components like a conversational interview agent, independent data retrieval, and a solver-agnostic Intermediate Representation (IR) that allows for deterministic recompilation across various solvers without further LLM calls. This approach addresses the limitations of academic tools by handling messy inputs and ensuring portability and reliability.
papers.figure_caption: ORPilot standard pipeline. Blue indicates an LLM-involved step, while orange indicates a deterministic step. Double arrows in opposite directions indicate the interactive nature of this step. Solid arrows represent unconditional transitions between steps, while dashed arrows represent conditional transitions between steps.

papers.title: Strategy-Aware Optimization Modeling with Reasoning LLMs
papers.abstract: This paper introduces SAGE, a framework that explicitly incorporates modeling strategies into the training of Large Language Models (LLMs) for optimization programming. SAGE utilizes a solver-verified, multi-strategy dataset and a Segment-Weighted GRPO fine-tuning approach with a composite reward focused on correctness and solver efficiency. This method significantly improves the LLM's ability to generate effective optimization formulations, boosting the average pass@1 rate and leading to more diverse and compact constraint systems.
papers.figure_caption: Why modeling strategy matters. A step-wise pipeline may define variables on an incorrect index space (e.g., ( A , A ) (A,A) ), creating invalid arcs and runtime failures (e.g., KeyError ). Strategy-aware reasoning first commits to a paradigm (e.g., flow-based) and restricts the decision domain (e.g., Links ), producing a consistent and solver-executable model.

papers.title: Beating the Style Detector: Three Hours of Agentic Research on the AI-Text Arms Race
papers.abstract: This paper demonstrates the efficiency of modern agentic research tools by reproducing and extending a recent NLP study in just three hours, with the human acting only as a reviewer. The core contribution is showing that state-of-the-art LLMs (GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.7) significantly close the style gap in text post-editing, achieving $71-75\%$ of the human author ceiling and outperforming human post-editing on most tasks. Furthermore, the work frames this capability as an "AI-text detection arms race," noting that current detection methods remain highly effective.

papers.title: Gradient-Gated DPO: Stabilizing Preference Optimization in Language Models
papers.abstract: The paper introduces **Gradient-Gated Preference Optimization (Gate-DPO)** to stabilize Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) training, which suffers from a "squeezing effect" causing probability collapse. Gate-DPO achieves this by introducing a gating mechanism that attenuates harmful gradients applied to rejected responses when the model is already assigning them extremely low probabilities. This modulation stabilizes training by preventing the over-suppression of alternative responses without sacrificing standard optimization behavior.
papers.figure_caption: Empirical demonstration of three structural pathologies in DPO. All experiments use Pythia-410M on Anthropic-HH (5 epochs). (a) Unbounded optimization: after the SFT → \( \rightarrow \) DPO transition, absolute log-probabilities drift downward despite preference learning. (b) Squeezing: probability mass concentrates on the argmax while both chosen and rejected responses decrease. (c) Valley collapse: low-probability regions are disproportionately suppressed under standard DPO.

papers.abstract: ContextualJailbreak introduces an evolutionary red-teaming strategy to automatically discover multi-turn jailbreak attacks that exploit contextual priming in LLMs. It performs evolutionary search over simulated conversational dialogues, using a two-level harm scoring system to guide the mutation process toward eliciting harmful responses. This method effectively automates the optimization of complex, multi-turn priming sequences, an area previously limited to manual crafting.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. End-to-end architecture of ContextualJailbreak . The pipeline generates contextual priming dialogues through an attacker model, tests them against a target LLM, and evaluates the responses via a two-stage judge system. Scored templates are then recycled to guide the ongoing evolutionary search.

papers.title: Reinforcement Learning for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems through Orchestration Traces
papers.abstract: This paper introduces "orchestration traces," temporal interaction graphs, as a framework to apply reinforcement learning (RL) to coordinate teams of LLM agents. The core method involves designing RL rewards and credit signals that specifically address the complex orchestration decisions—such as spawning, delegation, and aggregation—required for effective multi-agent collaboration. This work contributes a structured approach to optimize team-level performance beyond individual agent actions.

The core finding is that existing public skills primarily focus on workflow automation and monitoring, showing uneven coverage of the full clinical lifecycle and failing to adequately capture clinical risk compared to general technical risk. This work establishes the current state and critical gaps in reusable procedural components necessary for adapting AI agents across diverse healthcare settings.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. Distribution of healthcare skill size by token count (left) and file count (right).

Results indicate that approaches requiring extensive iterative agent reasoning lead to higher latency and more errors.

(a) A1 embeds the procedure within the agent, (b) A2 retrieves the procedure from an external database, (c) A3 receives the procedure in the input prompt, and (d) A4 encapsulates the procedure within a single tool. This approach ensures the compressed subspace is optimally aligned with downstream objectives.

papers.figure_caption: ReClaim framework and evaluation workflow. ( a ), Longitudinal medical events from patient claims are encoded as chronologically ordered trajectories, and the ReClaim foundation model autoregressively predicts future medical events including diagnoses, procedures, medications, and expenditure. ( b ), The study datasets comprise the MarketScan corpus partitioned into a final training set, a held-out internal test cohort for disease and expenditure prediction with retrospective and prospective temporal subsets, and two external testing datasets: EHRShot and Yale New Haven Health (YNHH). This partitioning and external sources enable four testing scenarios. hacker_news.title: How OpenAI delivers low-latency voice AI at scale
hacker_news.summary: OpenAI details the technical strategies, including optimized model architectures and efficient infrastructure, used to achieve low-latency, high-quality voice AI performance at a massive scale.

hacker_news.title: Google Chrome silently installs a 4 GB AI model on your device without consent
hacker_news.summary: Google Chrome is reportedly installing a large, 4GB AI model onto user devices without explicit consent, raising privacy concerns.

hacker_news.title: Train Your Own LLM from Scratch
hacker_news.summary: This GitHub project provides the necessary code and resources to train a custom Large Language Model entirely from the ground up.

hacker_news.title: Securing a DoD contractor: Finding a multi-tenant authorization vulnerability
hacker_news.summary: Strix discovered a critical multi-tenant authorization vulnerability in a Department of Defense contractor's system, allowing unauthorized access to sensitive data.

hacker_news.title: Let's talk about LLMs
hacker_news.summary: The article discusses Large Language Models (LLMs), likely exploring their current state, capabilities, or future implications.

hacker_news.title: OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft Back Bill to Fund 'AI Literacy' in Schools
hacker_news.summary: OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft are supporting a bipartisan bill aimed at funding "AI literacy" education within schools.

hacker_news.title: Sierra Raises $950M at $15B Valuation
hacker_news.summary: AI platform Sierra has successfully raised \$950 million in funding, achieving a \$15 billion valuation to further develop its technology for enhancing customer experiences.

github_trending.description: Coding agent for DeepSeek models that runs in your terminal
github_trending.summary: DeepSeek-TUI is a terminal-based coding agent that provides an interactive interface for interacting with DeepSeek models directly within your command line environment.

github_trending.description: 🌊 The leading agent orchestration platform for Claude. Deploy intelligent multi-agent swarms, coordinate autonomous workflows, and build conversational AI systems. Features enterprise-grade architecture, self-learning swarm intelligence, RAG integration, and native Claude Code / Codex Integration
github_trending.summary: ruflo is a leading agent orchestration platform for Claude that enables the deployment of intelligent, self-learning multi-agent swarms for autonomous workflows, featuring enterprise architecture, RAG integration, and native Claude Code/Codex support.

github_trending.description: An autonomous agent for deep financial research
github_trending.summary: Dexter is an autonomous agent designed for deep financial research, capable of performing complex analysis to uncover investment insights.

github_trending.description: Open source DocuSign alternative. Create, fill, and sign digital documents ✍️
github_trending.summary: DocuSeal is an open-source alternative to DocuSign, enabling users to create, fill out, and digitally sign electronic documents.

github_trending.description: VSCode theme based off the easemate IDE and Jetbrains islands theme
github_trending.summary: This GitHub repository provides a VS Code theme, "Dark Islands," inspired by the aesthetic of the Easemate IDE and JetBrains' Islands theme, offering a distinct visual experience for developers.

github_trending.description: Context window optimization for AI coding agents. Sandboxes tool output, 98% reduction. 14 platforms
github_trending.summary: Context-Mode optimizes AI coding agent context windows by sandboxing tool output, achieving a 98% reduction across 14 platforms.

github_trending.description: Incremental engine for long horizon agents 🌟 Star if you like it!
github_trending.summary: Cocoindex is an incremental engine designed to efficiently manage and process information for long-horizon agents.

github_trending.description: A complete AI agency at your fingertips - From frontend wizards to Reddit community ninjas, from whimsy injectors to reality checkers. Each agent is a specialized expert with personality, processes, and proven deliverables.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides a complete, multi-agent AI agency where specialized agents, each with a distinct personality and expertise (like frontend wizards or Reddit ninjas), collaborate to deliver proven results.

github_trending.description: A complete computer science study plan to become a software engineer.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides a comprehensive, structured computer science study plan designed to prepare individuals for software engineering interviews.
